beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and quick breakfast at home
beats.reasoning: Jackson's day always starts with a swim, but before that he needs to wake and prepare; folding morning routine into one beat as required.
Jackson can't start the day without a swim; this is his ritual and sets him up mentally for work. Short commute from home.
Drive from Northgate to Interbay; budget 40 minutes for cross-town movement and potential traffic on a hot Wednesday morning.

beats.activity: Counseling sessions and student check-ins at school
beats.reasoning: Morning and pre-lunch work block; counselor duties including sessions with students, follow-ups on referrals, and administrative tasks.

beats.activity: Lunch at school desk or nearby
beats.reasoning: Quick lunch at work; given the heat reaching 91F by afternoon and air quality concerns from wildfire smoke, staying at school is practical.

beats.activity: Afternoon counseling sessions and student support
beats.reasoning: Second half of work day; end-of-day sessions with students and documentation before commute home.
Drive from Interbay back to Northgate; 40-minute buffer accounts for end-of-day traffic and heat.

beats.activity: Stop by QFC for groceries and yarn supplies
beats.reasoning: Quick errand within neighborhood; Jackson regularly shops here for yarn and groceries. Stops early before evening heat and before the concert crowd builds.

beats.activity: Rest at home, hydrate, and prepare for evening
beats.reasoning: Break at home to cool down during the hottest part of the day (91F expected), drink water, and rest before deciding on the evening. Air quality concerns make staying indoors briefly advisable.

beats.activity: Drive to Gas Works Park for the outdoor concert
beats.reasoning: Jackson is spontaneous and enjoys being outdoors; the free concert at 6pm is appealing. Clear skies and cooling evening make this a good call. Budget 45 minutes from Northgate to Wallingford.

beats.activity: Enjoy outdoor concert at Gas Works Park
beats.reasoning: Jackson values time outdoors and spontaneous experiences; the concert fits his mood and the clear evening weather. Stays for about 1.5 hours to enjoy the music and breeze without getting caught in the largest crowd surge.

beats.activity: Drive home from Gas Works Park
beats.reasoning: Return commute from Wallingford to Northgate; evening traffic is lighter, but budget 45 minutes for safety.
Jackson winds down with his knitting ritual and a podcast; this is his pattern and settles him after a full day. Ensures he's home and relaxed before sleep.
Seven-hour sleep block ending before 6:30 am, allowing Jackson to wake and swim again tomorrow morning. Sleep beats span overnight as required.

diary:
Started the day early with my usual swim at Northgate Pool—the water was perfect, and I needed it after a busy week. Work at Interbay was steady; had a few meaningful check-ins with kids who've been struggling, which is why I do this job. The heat was building by lunch, so I stayed inside mostly, just picking at a sandwich at my desk.

After work I swung by QFC for groceries and grabbed some new yarn—been eyeing this merino blend for weeks. The afternoon felt long, so I rested up at home with plenty of water before heading out to Gas Works Park for the concert. The air quality wasn't great from those BC fires, but the music and the crowd lifted my spirits. There's something about live music outdoors that reminds me why I love this city. Came home, settled in with my knitting and a podcast, and let the day just fade away.
thought_process: The heat made me thoughtful today. I pushed myself a bit harder at the pool this morning—something about the temperature always energizes me. Work felt purposeful; I had a kid open up about their parents' divorce, and those moments anchor me. I almost skipped the concert because of the smoke, but I'm glad I didn't. The heat and air quality should've made me stay home, but I realized I needed that time outside, that reminder that life keeps happening even when conditions aren't perfect. By evening, I was genuinely tired in the good way—a full day that hit different parts of me. The knitting felt earned.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
